current_task: Phase 3 post-cutover soak window. Iter 133 wired the completion notifier into scripts/router-soak-hourly.sh as a final step (best-effort, doesn't override kill-switch rc), with ROUTER_SOAK_NOTIFY_SKIP=1 bypass for kill-switch drills. With this, a single registered hourly cron entry now drives all three pieces (soak monitor → kill-switch → completion notifier). All engineering pieces are in place. Phase 3 advancement is gated on (a) operator-side hourly cron registration (see scripts/router-soak-hourly.SKILL.md), and (b) ≥72h of clean PASS rollup accumulating from a future T0; no further engineering work can advance Phase 3 until those land.

tasks_completed_this_phase.rationale: Phase 3 exit criterion is '3-day soak passes all checks, kill-switch verified, documentation merged, **Telegram notification sent**'. Iter 131 shipped the rollup verifier that produces the PASS / INCOMPLETE verdict; the final 'Telegram notification sent' deliverable was still missing. This iteration ships the one-shot notifier: agent.router_soak_completion_notifier wraps router_soak_rollup.rollup(), and on the first transition to soak_complete=True writes a flag at logs/router_audit/phase3_soak_complete.json and Telegram-pushes a counts-only summary via the same agent.router_kill_switch.send_alert helper that all router alerts already use. Idempotent (re-runs while complete are no-ops); retry-on-send-failure (flag.sent=False is treated as not-yet-sent). Privacy boundary inherits from rollup — counts + ISO timestamps only, no query text or PII; explicit test asserts the message body contains no email/handle/password/imessage/imap markers.

review_passes.architectural: lives in agent/, depends only on agent.router_soak_rollup (verifier) and agent.router_kill_switch.send_alert (Telegram); no DB access, no Claude API, no subsystem imports; flag file lives under logs/router_audit/ alongside soak_*.json so the operator's existing audit-dir conventions cover it; idempotent — safe to fire from any cadence (hourly, build-agent loop, manual)
review_passes.self_simplify: ~115 LOC: one NotifyResult dataclass, one notify() entry, one _format_message helper, _read_flag/_write_flag helpers, async _send wrapper around the existing kill-switch helper, argparse main(); zero new external dependencies, no subsystem cross-imports
review_passes.test_coverage_integration: 13 tests: incomplete-window no-op, dry-run on complete window doesn't send/write, complete-window sends + writes flag, idempotent on prior sent=True, send-failure writes flag with sent=False so next run retries, corrupt flag treated as unsent, message body privacy contract (no @gmail/@pm.me/@yahoo/imessage/imap/password), CLI rc=0/1/2/5 paths, default flag path under audit dir

tasks_completed_this_phase.rationale: Migration plan §Phase 3 exit criterion is '3-day soak passes all checks'. Iter 125–130 delivered the per-hour soak monitor, router-p95 probe, kill-switch + drill, and hourly cron wrapper. Missing piece: the verification logic that turns a directory of soak_*.json hourly reports into a single PASS / INCOMPLETE verdict. This iteration ships that — agent.router_soak_rollup reads logs/router_audit/soak_*.json, filters to post-cutover artifacts, computes a contiguous-PASS check over the configured window (default 72h to match the spec), and exits 0 only when the soak window is complete. CLI is the same shape the kill-switch wrapper uses (rc=0 complete / rc=1 incomplete / rc=5 dir missing). Read-only — no DB, no Claude API, no external IO; output is counts + ISO timestamps + file paths only.

review_passes.architectural: lives in agent/, reads only on-disk JSON already produced by router_soak_monitor; no subsystem cross-imports; no DB access; no Claude API; no Telegram side effects; privacy boundary inherits from soak monitor (numeric counts + paths only)
review_passes.self_simplify: single ~190 LOC module, one dataclass + one rollup() + main(); no helpers beyond _parse_iso/_discover; CLI is argparse + dispatch
review_passes.test_coverage_integration: 16 tests: empty dir, only-pre-cutover, baseline file skipped, short-window incomplete, full-window completes, FAIL breaks contiguity, ROLLBACK breaks contiguity, unknown status breaks contiguity, --window-hours param respected, malformed JSON skipped, missing generated_at skipped, CLI rc=0/1/5 each, JSON output shape, real-dir smoke

operator_action_required.command: mcp__scheduled-tasks__create_scheduled_task with taskId=pepper-router-soak-hourly, cronExpression='0 * * * *', notifyOnCompletion=false, prompt=<body of scripts/router-soak-hourly.SKILL.md after '## Prompt body' header>
operator_action_required.reason: Claude scheduled-tasks runtime refuses `mcp__scheduled-tasks__create_scheduled_task` from inside a scheduled run (verified live this iter — error: 'Cannot create scheduled tasks from within a scheduled task session.'). The cron must be registered from an interactive session.
operator_action_required.verification: After registration, `mcp__scheduled-tasks__list_scheduled_tasks` shows pepper-router-soak-hourly with nextRunAt within the next hour.

tasks_completed_this_phase.rationale: Phase 3 spec calls for per-hour automated checks during the 3-day soak window (iter 125 baseline + soak monitor; iter 126 router-p95 probe; iter 127 kill-switch). This task closes the loop: a single cron entry that runs the soak monitor then dispatches the kill-switch on its result. Wrapper lives in scripts/ (not agent/) because it is bash plumbing that calls into agent.* modules — keeps agent/* a pure Python package per project conventions. Companion SKILL.md is canonical because the Claude scheduled-tasks runtime refuses `mcp__scheduled-tasks__create_scheduled_task` from inside a scheduled run (discovered live this iter), so the operator must register the cron from an interactive session — keeping the prompt in-repo means it's not lost.

tasks_completed_this_phase.rationale: Migration plan §Phase 3 specifies: 'Telegram ping on any check fail' + 'Auto-rollback (docker rebuild from archive) if eval drops below 80%'. Iter 125–126 landed the soak monitor (read-only); this run wires the side-effecting actions on top. Default behaviour is plan-only (Hard Rule 1: plan + snapshot persisted before any destructive command); auto-execution is gated by ROUTER_KILL_SWITCH_CONFIRM=I-UNDERSTAND-DOCKER-REBUILD so an unattended scheduled run cannot accidentally rebuild the stack on a transient eval blip — operator arms after reviewing the plan.json + Telegram alert.

tasks_completed_this_phase.rationale: Iter 125 left the p95_router_latency_ms soak gate as SKIP because the routing_events.latency_ms column measures the whole chat turn, not the router alone. Spec calls for a router-only <200ms p95 gate every hour. This task wires SemanticRouter into the existing pluggable router_p95_runner via a closure-built runner that reuses router_latency_bench.bench, then calls it by default from the CLI. Imports are deferred to the runner body so the unit test suite (and any caller that supplies its own runner) doesn't pull the LLM/embedding chain at import time.

review_passes.architectural: runner imports inside the closure (no top-level coupling); reuses bench/load_queries/percentile from router_latency_bench; queries come from canonical eval set (no PII); no Claude API / subsystem cross-imports; pluggable surface preserved
